evidence: These systems require an ongoing commitment of significant resources to maintain, protect, and enhance existing systems and develop and integrate new systems, including systems powered by or incorporating artificial intelligence and machine learning (including generative AI)
automate and deploy new technologies to simplify administrative processes and clinical decision making
automate and deploy new technologies to simplify administrative processes and clinical decision making
develop new and innovative products and services (including enhanced technologies that improve) ... systems powered by or incorporating AI/ML

changes to laws, regulations and guidance regarding how we may use AI/ML could make it harder for us to conduct our business using AI/ML, require us to retrain our AI/ML, delete data produced by our AI/ML

if our use of AI/ML technologies were to result in inaccuracies, biases or errors, we could have operational disruptions, problems in determining medical cost estimates and establishing appropriate pricing
techniques used to obtain unauthorized access ... are increasing in sophistication, in part due to use of evolving AI/ML technologies (including generative AI)

limitations: ai_investment_intensity: no dollar amounts, headcount, or quantified AI-specific spend disclosed in the excerpts; named commitment is qualitative only

ai_narrative_concreteness: excerpts are drawn entirely from risk-factor sections; no operational or business-results sections are present, limiting visibility into deployed use cases or quantified outcomes
Promotional language absent; risk-factor framing means AI references are cautionary rather than aspirational, which may understate actual deployment concreteness described elsewhere in the filing
